title: Wallets nav item leads to 404 — /wallets route not implemented
description:
CRITICAL: Clicking the "Wallets" sidebar nav item (visible to every logged-in user) navigates to /wallets, which returns a raw HTTP 404 rendered by SvelteKit's default unstyled error page ("404 / Not Found") — no Cairn branding, no nav, no way back except browser back button.

Repro: log in, click "Wallets" in the sidebar, or GET /wallets directly.

Root cause: no src/routes/(app)/wallets/** route exists yet (confirmed via glob — only +layout, explorer, settings, admin, and the app-level +page exist under src/routes/(app)/). Backend groundwork is partially in place (src/lib/server/wallets.ts, src/lib/server/bitcoin/walletScan.ts exist) but no frontend page/actions were wired up.

Impact: blocks the entire "Wallets" feature area for every user — import wizard, wallet detail, receive-address/QR generation are all untestable and unusable right now. The nav item should either be removed/hidden until the feature ships, or (better) the route should be built.

Also file separately: the default 404 error page itself is unbranded and should be replaced with a Cairn-styled error page app-wide.
close_reason: Fixed: wallet navigator implemented in 5595b82 — /wallets, import wizard, detail, and API all live and verified

title: Admin
description: Instance administration: user management, invite management, registration policy, and node connection configuration with public-server defaults.
close_reason: All admin children complete in 51b674b

title: Admin overview
description: Instance health at a glance: user/wallet/invite counts, registration mode, and live node connection status (mode, server, chain tip).
close_reason: Implemented in 51b674b: admin overview with user/wallet/invite counts, registration mode, live node status

title: User management
description: List all users with role, status, wallet count, and activity timestamps. Enable/disable accounts and grant/revoke admin, with protection against removing the last administrator.
close_reason: Implemented in 51b674b: user list with enable/disable and promote/demote, last-admin protection in src/lib/server/admin.ts

title: Invite management
description: Create invites singly or in batches with optional label, per-code use limits, and expiry. List active/exhausted/expired/revoked codes with one-click copy, and revoke active codes.
close_reason: Implemented in 51b674b: single/batch invite creation with labels, use limits, expiry; revocation; copy-to-clipboard codes

title: Instance settings
description: Registration mode (open/invite/closed) and node connection configuration: public servers by default or custom Electrum host/port/TLS, Esplora URL, and optional Bitcoin Core RPC credentials. Connection tests for each backend; changes apply live without a restart.
close_reason: Implemented in 51b674b: registration mode, public/custom connection config with Electrum+Esplora tests, live reconfiguration without restart

title: Signup form: native browser validation silently blocks submit with no visible error (required invite code field)
description:
On /signup, all four fields (displayName, email, password, inviteCode when shown) use bare HTML `required` attributes with no custom validation messaging. If a user tries to submit with any field empty/invalid, the browser's native tooltip validation kicks in (e.g. "Please fill out this field.") — there is no app-level error state, no red outline beyond the native browser default, and nothing in Cairn's own visual language. This is inconsistent with the polished custom "form-error" banner the page already has for server-side errors (see form?.error in +page.svelte).

Repro: go to /signup, leave "Invite code" blank (when invite-only registration mode requires it), fill everything else, click "Create account" — the button appears to do nothing except show the browser's default native validation bubble, easily mistaken for the button being broken.

Suggested fix: add client-side validation with Cairn-styled inline error messages instead of relying solely on native `required` attributes.
close_reason: Fixed in 1f94da6: signup form validates in the enhance handler (novalidate) and renders failures in the app form-error style, covering the required invite code case

title: .claude/launch.json committed to git (internal tooling exposed)
description: Commit efd7bac added .claude/launch.json to the tracked file set. .claude/ is internal Claude Code tooling config and must not be exposed in a public OSS repo per project standards. Need to: (1) git rm --cached .claude/launch.json, (2) add .claude/ to .gitignore, (3) confirm no other .claude/ artifacts get committed going forward (also watch for .beads/ which is now initialized and must be handled per project policy on whether the tracker itself is meant to be public).
close_reason: Fixed: .claude/launch.json untracked via git rm --cached and .claude/ ignored in cb72bcc. Decision on .beads/: it is committed intentionally as the shared issue tracker (beads is designed for git sync)

title: README.md is unedited sv/SvelteKit scaffold boilerplate
description: README.md still contains the default 'sv create' template content (generic 'Creating a project' / 'Developing' / 'Building' sections referencing npx sv create). It has no project description, no explanation of what Cairn is, no setup instructions specific to this app (env vars, data dir, invite-based registration, electrum/esplora config), no screenshots. A public-facing OSS repo needs a real README from commit one.
close_reason: Fixed: README rewritten with project description, features, stack, run instructions, first-run guide, and node configuration notes (commit 76d7ffa/HEAD lineage; see README.md)

title: Commit messages don't use conventional-commit prefixes
description: All 3 commits so far (e42a7b5, efd7bac, 8582c31) use plain imperative titles ('Add database layer and session auth', 'Add Bitcoin server library') with no type prefix. Project standard calls for conventional format: 'feat: ...', 'fix: ...', 'refactor: ...'. The messages themselves are detailed and professional (not lazy/vague) — this is purely about adopting the type: prefix convention going forward, not a rewrite of existing history.
close_reason: Adopted: conventional-commit prefixes in use from 1b1caab (feat: add block explorer) onward; earlier history left untouched per the issue's own recommendation

title: Infrastructure
description: Foundation everything else depends on: application scaffold, design system, Electrum client, authentication, database schema, and brand assets.
close_reason: All infrastructure children complete

title: Application scaffold and routing
description: SvelteKit + TypeScript scaffold with adapter-node, route groups for authenticated and unauthenticated areas, and the app shell with sidebar navigation.
close_reason: Implemented in e42a7b5: SvelteKit scaffold, adapter-node, route groups, app shell with sidebar nav

title: Design system
description: Design tokens (warm charcoal surfaces, copper accent, status colors), Inter for UI text with Fraunces for display numerals, and shared component styles: cards, buttons, forms, tables, badges, and state patterns (loading, empty, error).

title: Electrum client
description: Electrum protocol client over TCP/TLS: JSON-RPC with request pipelining, header and scripthash subscriptions, graceful reconnection with backoff, and server switching at runtime. Paired with an Esplora-compatible HTTP source for block and mempool detail the Electrum protocol cannot serve.
close_reason: Implemented in 8582c31: Electrum TCP/TLS client with pipelining, subscriptions, backoff reconnect, runtime reconfiguration; Esplora HTTP source with mempool.space enrichment

title: Authentication and sessions
description: Email/password authentication with scrypt hashing, database-backed sessions delivered via httpOnly cookies (revocable server-side, unlike stateless tokens), invite redemption, and registration policy enforcement.
close_reason: Implemented in efd7bac: scrypt hashing, DB-backed httpOnly cookie sessions, invite redemption, registration modes. Sessions chosen over stateless JWT so admin disable revokes access immediately

title: Database schema
description: SQLite via node:sqlite (no native addons): users, sessions, wallets, invites, and settings tables with idempotent schema creation, WAL mode, and foreign keys.
close_reason: Implemented in efd7bac: node:sqlite schema (users, sessions, wallets, invites, settings), WAL, foreign keys, idempotent creation

title: Logo and favicon
description: Cairn mark: three stacked stones in the copper accent, legible at 16px, used as the favicon and in the sidebar/auth wordmark lockups.
close_reason: Implemented in e42a7b5: three-stone cairn mark in copper, favicon.svg + Logo component

title: Polish pass
description: Cross-cutting quality pass before release: loading and error states on every network-dependent view, empty states, responsive behavior on small screens, and keyboard/AT affordances on interactive elements.
close_reason: Completed across 888de27, 1f94da6, c017a84: loading/error/empty states on all network-dependent views, responsive sweep verified at 375px on dashboard, explorer, mempool, block, tx, address, and wallet detail, branded error page, form validation in app style, production build passing

title: OP_RETURN output labeled 'Unspent' — should be Unspendable/N-A
description:
On a transaction detail page, an OP_RETURN output is shown with status "Unspent", the same label used for genuinely spendable-but-not-yet-spent outputs. OP_RETURN outputs are provably unspendable by consensus rules, so labeling one "Unspent" is technically incorrect and actively misleading for an educational explorer — a user could reasonably conclude the funds there could still be spent.

Repro: visit /explorer/tx/caca5a48c62c196940b1236624e816475f8bafa582619ae25ff53d7a5c3d9731 (coinbase tx in block 956,591) and look at the second output (OP_RETURN, 0.00 BTC) — it shows "Unspent" like the first output.

Suggested fix: special-case OP_RETURN outputs to show "Unspendable" (or omit the spent/unspent badge entirely and show the OP_RETURN data instead).
close_reason: Fixed in 1f94da6: OP_RETURN outputs now show an Unspendable badge with an explanation instead of Spent/Unspent

title: No dedicated mempool page with fee distribution histogram
description:
The Dashboard and Explorer pages both show mempool summary stats (unconfirmed tx count, mempool fees total, mempool size in MB, and a 4-tier recommended-fee table: Fastest/Half hour/Hour/Economy), but there is no dedicated /mempool page with a visual fee-distribution histogram (e.g. sat/vB buckets vs. vsize, the classic mempool.space-style chart). Confirmed via route glob and a direct 404 on GET /mempool.

This may be intentional/deferred scope rather than a bug, but flagging since the original test plan called out "mempool page — fee distribution, recommended fees" as an expected surface. Recommended fees ARE present and look correct; only the distribution visualization is missing.
close_reason: Fixed in 0046d11: /explorer/mempool with 7-band fee distribution, projected next blocks, contextual fee recommendations, and 2h backlog sparkline, linked from the explorer mempool strip

title: Bitcoin Core RPC password round-trips to the client in plaintext
description: src/lib/server/settings.ts getInstanceSettings() returns coreRpcPass in plaintext. This flows straight into: (1) src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.server.ts load(), which SvelteKit serializes into the SSR page payload and (2) src/routes/(app)/admin/settings/+page.svelte, which pre-fills a type=password input with value={data.settings.coreRpcPass}. It's also returned as plaintext JSON from GET /api/admin/settings (src/routes/api/admin/settings/+server.ts). Anyone with view-source/devtools access to an authenticated admin session (or anything that logs the page payload/API response) can read the stored Core RPC credential. Fix: never send the stored secret back to the client — return a boolean 'has value' flag or a masked placeholder instead, and only overwrite the stored value when the admin submits a non-empty new one.
close_reason: Fixed in cb72bcc: introduced getPublicInstanceSettings() replacing the stored Core RPC password with a hasCoreRpcPass flag; admin settings load, page markup, and GET /api/admin/settings no longer serialize the secret; blank submissions keep the stored value, with an explicit clear path

title: Wallet Navigator

close_reason: All wallet navigator children complete in 5595b82

title: Wallet list page
description: Cards for each imported wallet: name, script type, confirmed balance, pending delta, and last activity. Empty state introduces watch-only wallets and routes into the import wizard.
close_reason: Implemented in 5595b82: wallet grid with balances, script badges, pending/unreachable indicators, onboarding empty state

title: Wallet import wizard
description: Guided five-step import: choose type (watch-only xpub), paste the extended public key with a plain-language explanation of what an xpub is and is not, preview derived addresses for verification against the source wallet, name the wallet, and land on the populated wallet view. Server-side validation with friendly errors.
close_reason: Implemented in 5595b82: five-step guided import (type, key with xpub explainer, derived-address preview, name, done banner) with server-side validation

title: Wallet detail page
description: Balance hero (confirmed + pending), transaction history with direction and net amounts, and an address inventory split into used/unused with derivation paths, per-address balances, and usage counts.
close_reason: Implemented in 5595b82: balance hero, transactions and used/unused address tabs with derivation paths, two-click delete

title: Receive address generation with QR
description: Serve the next unused receive address with a QR code, derivation path, and copy affordance. Advancing stays within the BIP44 gap limit so funds are always visible to recovering wallets.
close_reason: Implemented in 5595b82: QR receive card with gap-limit-clamped next-address generation, verified live (m/0/40 -> m/0/41)

title: Default unstyled SvelteKit error page shown on 404 (no Cairn branding)
description:
Any unmatched route (e.g. /wallets right now, or any typo'd URL) renders SvelteKit's bare default error page: a plain "404" heading and "Not Found" paragraph, no styling, no Cairn logo/sidebar, no link back into the app.

Expected: a branded error page consistent with The Forge design system (warm charcoal background, copper accent, Cairn logo, a "Back to Dashboard" or "Back to sign in" link depending on auth state) — matching the quality bar set by other pages (e.g. the empty/closed-registration state on /signup which IS well designed).

Repro: navigate to any non-existent route, e.g. http://localhost:<port>/wallets or /explorer/foo/bar.
close_reason: Fixed in 1f94da6: branded +error.svelte on The Forge background with logo, status hero, and links back to the dashboard/explorer. Verified live on an unmatched route

title: .gitignore missing .claude/ entry
description: Current .gitignore has no entry for .claude/, which is how .claude/launch.json ended up committed (see linked bug). Add '.claude/' to .gitignore to prevent recurrence.
close_reason: Fixed: .claude/ added to .gitignore in cb72bcc, along with .idea/, logs, and OS artifacts

title: No educational tooltips anywhere in the explorer (block/tx/address jargon unexplained)
description:
Cairn is framed as an educational Bitcoin explorer, but block/tx/address detail pages surface raw protocol jargon with zero explanation: DIFFICULTY, NONCE, BITS, VERSION, WEIGHT, MERKLE ROOT on block pages; LOCKTIME on tx pages; address type codes like "P2WSH"/"P2WPKH" on address pages. None of these have hover tooltips, info icons, or inline help text.

Verified via code search: `grep -ri "tooltip|educational|help-text|info-icon"` across src/ returns zero matches — there is no tooltip infrastructure in the codebase at all.

Repro: visit /explorer/block/<any height> and hover over "DIFFICULTY", "NONCE", "BITS" labels — nothing happens. Same for address type badge on /explorer/address/<addr>.

Impact: for an app whose differentiator is explaining Bitcoin to newcomers, this is a significant gap versus the stated goal, not just a nice-to-have.
close_reason: Fixed in 0046d11: Term tooltip component with dotted-underline affordance now explains nonce, difficulty, bits, merkle root, weight, fees, vsize, locktime, RBF, SegWit, address types, and more across block/tx/address pages, plus collapsible How-does-this-work panels per page

title: No LICENSE file in public repo
description: Cairn is a public open-source repo but has no LICENSE file. Task spec requires MIT license from commit one. Add a standard MIT LICENSE file at the repo root with the correct copyright holder/year.
close_reason: Fixed: MIT LICENSE added at repo root in cb72bcc

title: Explorer
description: Block explorer covering blocks, transactions, addresses, the mempool, fee estimation, and universal search. The centerpiece of v1: every piece of data should be transparent and educational, so that someone new to Bitcoin understands what they are looking at after a few minutes of browsing.
close_reason: All explorer children complete: list, block, tx, address, mempool, search, educational framework

title: Block list page
description: Paginated list of recent blocks: height, age, transaction count, size, and fee range. Links into block detail. Height-based pagination (newer/older).
close_reason: Implemented in 1b1caab: paginated blocks list with height, age, tx count, size, fee range

title: Block detail page with educational annotations
description: Full block view: height, hash, timestamp, size, weight, transaction count, difficulty, nonce, bits, version, and merkle root — each technical field carries a plain-language explanation (e.g. nonce: the number miners searched for to solve this block's puzzle; merkle root: a fingerprint of every transaction in the block). Includes an introductory 'what is a block' panel, miner/pool identification from the coinbase, fee statistics (total, average rate, range) with an explanation of why fees exist, a highlighted coinbase transaction with subsidy + fees = reward breakdown, and a confirmations count explaining why more confirmations mean more settlement assurance. Paginated transaction list, coinbase first.
close_reason: Implemented in 1b1caab + 0046d11: full block view with plain-language tooltips on every header field, what-is-a-block panel, pool identification, subsidy+fees reward breakdown, coinbase explanation, confirmations context. Verified live on block 800000 (Carbon Negative, 6.25+0.1368768=6.3868768 BTC)

title: Transaction detail page with input/output flow
description: Transaction view built around the UTXO model: inputs on the left, outputs on the right, visually connected, with an explanation that Bitcoin tracks unspent outputs rather than account balances. Explains change outputs by analogy, shows fee as the input/output difference with sat/vB rate, indicates SegWit usage and the size vs virtual-size distinction, flags RBF (sequence-based) replaceability, and renders confirmation status as a 0-6 progress meter. Unconfirmed transactions get an ETA based on current fee rates.
close_reason: Implemented in 1b1caab + 0046d11: inputs-left/outputs-right UTXO flow with consumed/created framing, change-output detection with explanation, fee as in-minus-out, SegWit and RBF indicators, size vs vsize tooltip, 0-6 confirmation meter, fee-based ETA for unconfirmed

title: Address detail page with type detection
description: Address view that identifies and explains the address format (Legacy 1..., Nested SegWit 3..., Native SegWit bc1q..., Taproot bc1p...) with relative cost/efficiency context. Shows confirmed vs unconfirmed balance, total received/sent, transaction count, first/last seen dates, and a history table with per-transaction deltas and a running balance.
close_reason: Implemented in 1b1caab + 0046d11: address format detection with cost/efficiency explanations (Legacy/P2SH/SegWit/Taproot), confirmed vs unconfirmed with tooltips, total received/sent, first/last seen, history with running balance-after column

title: Mempool page
description: Dedicated mempool view introducing the mempool as Bitcoin's waiting room: unconfirmed transaction count, total size and fees, a fee-rate distribution visualization, recommended fee tiers with concrete time/cost framing, and projected upcoming blocks showing what will likely confirm next based on fee rate.
close_reason: Implemented in 0046d11: /explorer/mempool with waiting-room explainer, live stats (91k txs, 41.7MB verified), 7-band fee distribution, six projected blocks with fee ranges, contextual fee guidance, 2h backlog sparkline; degrades gracefully on plain esplora

title: Universal search
description: Search that classifies input (block height, block hash, transaction id, address), states what it detected rather than silently redirecting, offers guidance when input is ambiguous or invalid, and remembers the user's recent searches.
close_reason: Implemented in 1b1caab + 0046d11: classification states what it detected (looks-like-a-X card with view button) instead of silently redirecting, guidance on unclassifiable input, per-user recent searches with clear control

title: Educational UI framework
description: Cross-cutting components the educational explorer depends on: a term-explanation tooltip (dotted underline affordance, hover/tap to reveal), a collapsible 'How does this work?' panel with per-page copy that remembers open/closed state, and consistent number/timestamp formatting (BTC to 8 decimals, sats with separators, relative + absolute times).
close_reason: Implemented in 0046d11: Term tooltip component (dotted underline, hover/focus), HowItWorks collapsible panel with per-page persisted state, ConfirmMeter; consistent BTC/sats/relative+absolute time formatting via lib/format

title: Possible race condition: rapid 'Test connection' clicks in admin node settings can silently drop results / reset form
description:
While testing Admin > Settings > Node connection (Custom mode), I observed the following once and could not reliably reproduce it on retry, so filing at low confidence for someone with more time to investigate:

Sequence: switch connection mode to "Custom" (client-side only, not yet saved) -> click "Test connection" next to Electrum server (got "Connected — tip N") -> immediately click "Test connection" next to the Esplora base URL field -> the whole Node connection section reverted to "Public servers" and the Custom-mode fields (Electrum server, Esplora URL, Bitcoin Core RPC) disappeared entirely, discarding the unsaved mode selection.

On a clean retry of the same two-rapid-clicks sequence, the mode did NOT fully reset, but the second ("Esplora") test-connection's result text was silently dropped (button reverted to plain "Test connection" instead of showing "OK — tip N") — suggesting a race between the two forms' `use:enhance` submissions where a slower response overwrites a faster one, or an `invalidateAll()`/reload triggered by one form's success handler stomps the other's in-flight local state.

Suggested investigation: check whether the "Test connection" buttons' form actions call `invalidateAll()` or otherwise trigger the parent `+page.server.ts` load to re-run and overwrite local $state (connectionMode, per-field test results) with stale server data, especially when two test-connection requests are in flight concurrently.
close_reason: Fixed in 1f94da6: per-backend test results are copied into local state as they arrive, so the shared ActionData slot can no longer drop or overwrite the other test's badge; buttons stay disabled while a test is in flight. The one-off full mode reset was not reproducible; local-state preservation removes the suspected stomp path

title: Onboarding
description: Account creation and first-contact experience: signup with invite codes, first-user-becomes-admin flow, and orientation for new users after login.
close_reason: All onboarding children complete: auth pages, first-run, nav tour

title: First-run experience

close_reason: Implemented in efd7bac: empty instance redirects to signup, first account becomes admin without invite, explanatory note shown

title: Post-login navigation tour
description: Lightweight one-time orientation after a user's first login pointing out the dashboard, explorer, wallets, and settings. Dismissible and never shown again once completed.
close_reason: Implemented in 0046d11: dismissible one-time welcome card on the dashboard pointing to Explorer, Wallets, and Mempool; dismissal persisted per account
